Object.Name: Actuator
Object.Description1: Generic actuator control for valves, power switch, mower, etc.

Item.Name: default_duration_seconds
Item.Description: Duration of action started with a physical button. Setting to 0 disables the start on button press.

Item.Name: available
Item.Description: Indicate whether this actuator is available (e.g. valve connected)

Item.Name: paused_until
Item.Description: Scheduled actions paused until seconds from unix epoch

Item.Name: name
Item.Description: Name to be used for homekit?

Item.Name: master
Item.Description: 0:NONE when not linked to master channel. 1:LINKED if linked to master channel.

Item.Name: state
Item.Description: 0:INACTIVE this actuator is currently neither ACTIVE nor performs a state transition 1:STARTING this actuator is transitioning to the *_ACTIVE state 2:ACTIVE this actuator is currently running but will stop in case of power loss 3:STOPPING this actuator is currently leaving an *_ACTIVE state and should soon become INACTIVE

Item.Name: start
Item.Description: Start action of this actuator and create a new timeslot corresponding to this action. Takes two arguments: 0: integer: start source. The only purpose is to be copied over to corresponding timeslot. You can see list of values in Timeslot/*/initial_source. Use value 2 if not sure. 1: integer: duration in seconds. Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ poetry run bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" actuator/0/start "as=[0='10',1='60']"

Item.Name: stop
Item.Description: Immediately stop actuator. Takes one argument: 0: integer: stop source. Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ poetry run bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" actuator/0/stop "as=[0='10']"
